exercises.title: Transform with Comprehensions
exercises.description: Using list comprehensions (not for loops), create: a list of squares for numbers 1-8, a list of uppercased words from the given list, and a list of word lengths.
exercises.starterCode:
words = ["python", "is", "great", "and", "powerful"]

# TODO: Create 'squares' = squares of numbers 1 through 8 using a comprehension

# TODO: Create 'upper_words' = each word in uppercase using a comprehension

# TODO: Create 'lengths' = length of each word using a comprehension

print(squares)
print(upper_words)
print(lengths)
hints: [n**2 for n in range(1, 9)] creates squares
[w.upper() for w in words] uppercases each word
[len(w) for w in words] gets each word's length

exercises.title: Filter with Conditions
exercises.description: Using conditional list comprehensions: filter only even numbers from 1-20, label each grade as 'pass' or 'fail' (pass = 60+), and extract long words (length > 4) in uppercase.
exercises.starterCode:
grades = [85, 52, 92, 43, 78, 95, 61]
words = ["python", "java", "go", "rust", "javascript", "c"]

# TODO: Create 'evens' = even numbers from 1 to 20 using a comprehension

# TODO: Create 'results' = 'pass' if grade >= 60 else 'fail' for each grade

# TODO: Create 'long_upper' = uppercase words longer than 4 characters

print(evens)
print(results)
print(long_upper)
hints: [n for n in range(1, 21) if n % 2 == 0] filters evens
['pass' if g >= 60 else 'fail' for g in grades] uses a ternary in the comprehension
[w.upper() for w in words if len(w) > 4] combines filter and transform

exercises.title: Nested Comprehension: Multiplication Table
exercises.description: Use a nested list comprehension to build a 4x4 multiplication table as a list of lists, then print each row.
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Create 'table' as a 4x4 multiplication table using a nested comprehension
# table[i][j] should equal (i+1) * (j+1) for i, j in range(4)
# Expected: [[1,2,3,4],[2,4,6,8],[3,6,9,12],[4,8,12,16]]


# TODO: Print each row of the table on its own line
hints: Outer comprehension: [... for row in range(1, 5)]
Inner comprehension: [row * col for col in range(1, 5)]
Combined: [[row * col for col in range(1, 5)] for row in range(1, 5)]
Use a for loop to print each row

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Comprehension Syntax
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace through both comprehensions carefully.
warmup.starterCode:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
result1 = [n * 2 for n in nums if n % 2 == 0]
result2 = ["even" if n % 2 == 0 else "odd" for n in nums]
print(result1)
print(result2)
